text: After adequate bowel positioning, dissection of the inferior mesentric vein is started.
Dissection in the correct plane is required to ensure preservation and safety of the retropaternal nerve structures.
After dissection of the inferior mesenteric vein, now the dissection of inferior mesenteric artery is begun.
Dissection begins from the region of the rectovesicle fold just above the level of the ureter.
Scoring just below the level of the inferior mesenteric artery helps enter the correct plane.
During this phase of dissection, all the care is taken to keep the hypogastric nerves
down and to stay just below the level of the inferior mesenteric artery.
Detecting just outside the glistening envelope of the mesorectum helps us ensure safety of the hypogastric nerves.
The presacral fascia contains the vascular and the neural plexus and any breach of the fascia can lead to bleeding and injury to the nerves.
Moving away from the initial retrorectal dissection, we approach the IMA route.
This is the area where the pre-aortic superior hypogastric plexus converges to form two hypogastric nerves.
Care is taken to stay just below the level of the inferior mesenteric artery and to drop all the nerves below.
Any discrete lymph nodes in the pre-aortic region below the level of the inferior mesenteric artery are removed meticulously, avoiding any injury to the neural plexus here.
The IMA and the IMV are then grabbed by the assistance graspers to create the so-called popular Siegel sign.
The yellow shaded area here represents
the fibro fatty lymph node bearing tissue which have to be removed completely to ensure oncological adequacy.
The nodes at the root of the inferior mesentric artery are being removed and we approach the root.
Care is taken to avoid injury to all the entire neural plexus surrounding the root of the IMA.
After a meticulous dissection here, we can see
the preserved neural plexus around the region of the IMA root.
Following a meticulous dissection, the IMA is clipped not flush to the aorta but a centimeter or two beyond the root.
Medial to lateral dissection is then done in the correct plane, keeping the white line of toll in vision.
Following the mobilization of the descending colon, now the lateral peritoneal cut is taken.
Plenic fracture mobilization is then done to gain enough colonic length for a run anastomosis later
Having completed the colonic phase of surgery, we now move to the rectal phase.
Anterior rectal dissection is begun by incising the peritoneum just outside the rectovesicle fold.
As we go deep, care is taken to avoid any injury to the seminal vesicles.

The parasympathetic nerves from S2 to S4 along with the sympathetic component from the hypogastric nerve form the inferior hypogastric plexus which is at risk of injury during this phase of dissection.
Care is taken to stay exactly in the region between the denonvillous fascia and mesorectum.
The denonvillous fascia is then incised to approach the rectal tube.
After completing the anterior dissection now the lateral dissection is started.
During this lateral dissection care is taken to stay just outside the mesorectum and not towards the lateral wall to avoid any injury to the neural structure.
Dissection on the right side is then followed by a lateral dissection on the left side.
Here we can see that by meticulous dissection we ensure taking only the supply of the rectum and not of the remaining pelvic viscera.
Having done the lateral dissection on both the sides, we now approach the remainder of the posterior dissection.
Here we can nicely see the two hypogastric nerves and the S2 to S4 sacral nerves.
We continue this posterior dissection till we reach the pelvic floor.
A thorough and complete defattening of the rectal tube is done on all the sides.
Ensuring an adequate longitudinal margin, the rectal tube is stapled off using an endo stapler.
Now after the removal of the specimen, we can clearly see the two hypogastric nerves medial to both the ureters and then
The S2, S3 and S4 nerves on both the sides.
A stapled colloanal anastomosis is performed using a circular stapler.
